titleStmt.title: SII 40.120: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 120. (A.R. No. B. 99 of 1930.) Erraguḍipāḍu, Kamalapuram Taluk, Cuddapah District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On the south wall of the Kēśavasvāmi temple in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1471, Saumya, Phālguṇa, ba. 30, Solar eclipse, corresponding to 1550 A.D., March 18, Tuesday.

Damaged. Registers the grant of land at Yeṟṟaguḍipāḍu in Ghaṇḍikōṭa-sīma to the temple of Narasiṁha by Tammarāja, son of Tirumalayadēva-Mahārāja of the Ātrēya-gōtra. The Ghaṇḍikōṭa-sīma is stated to have been given to Nandēla Timmayadēva Mahārāja by the king as nāyaṁkara.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakha varuṣaṁbhulu 1471
Agunēṭi sauṁmya saṁvatsara phālguṇa ba 30 lu śrīmadrājādhirāja rājaparamēśvara śrīvīrapratāpa
śrī vīrasadāsivadēvamahārāyalu vidhyānagaramaṁdu siṁhāsanārūṁḍhulai pṛthivī sāṁbrājyaṁsēyu
cuṁḍaṁgānu Akhilāṁḍakōṭi brahmāṁḍa nāyakulaina dēvadēvōttamulaina Animela śrīkoṁḍa siṁgari śrī narasiṁha dēvara divya śrī caraṇā
raviṁdayugalaṁbulakunu Ātrēyasa gōtra Āpastaṁbha sūtra yajuśśākhādhyāyulaina sōmavaṁśōdbhavulaina tirumala
dēva mahārājuluṁgāri putrulu taṁma rājugāru daṁḍaṁ samarpiṁci yiccina bhūdāna dhaṁrmaśāsana kramameṭlaṁnanu sadāsivadēva mahārā
yalugāru naṁdēla tiṁmayadēva mahārājulu vāriki nayaṁkarānaku pāliṁcanavadharimcina ghaṁḍikōṭasīma[Avuku]maya ni jellē yaraguḍipāṭi
grāmaṁlōnu tadhātithini sūryōparāga puṇyakālamaṁdu na śrī nārasiṁhvadēvuni saṁnitini ni mela koṁ gā śrī narasi
samappiṁciti tūrppu dharmaṁ Akṣarālā mu
yatimiti cira cenu nādhara cirāpūrva śrīma
siddhasādhyaṁbulu Aṣṭa
samarpiṁcina dhānadharma śāsanamu . svadattā dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ paradattānu
pālanaṁ paradattāpahārēṇa svadattaṁ niṣphalaṁ bhavēt
svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vvāyō harēti vasuṁdharāṁ śaṣṭirvvarṣa sahasrāṇiṁ viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi .
tāmara nārāyaṇa maṁgaḷamahā śrī
Digital edition of SII 40.120 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
